title: node-postgres Pool, Client Ownership, Transactions, and Shutdown · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for node-postgres pool, client ownership, transactions, and shutdown and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded pg repository and transaction port. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Create one bounded Pool, handle background errors, observe acquire waits, reject work during closure, and await pool.end after workers settle.
Release every successfully acquired client in finally, preserve the original error, and test error, cancellation, and shutdown paths.
Run BEGIN, every statement, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on the same checked-out client and prohibit pool.query inside the unit of work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
